workflow.name: Monitor news sentiment trends with GPT-4o-mini, MrScraper, Slack and Notion
workflow.description:
## Description

This n8n template automatically monitors news sources daily, analyzes article sentiment using AI, and delivers structured intelligence reports to your team — all without any manual reading. It uses MrScraper to discover and extract articles, GPT-4o-mini to score sentiment and flag urgent issues, and delivers results to both Notion (for archiving) and Slack (for real-time alerts and daily digests).

Whether you're tracking brand reputation, monitoring a competitor, or staying on top of industry trends, this workflow turns the open web into a fully automated radar system that runs every morning before your team starts their day.

---

## How It Works

* **Phase 1 – Trigger & Config:** A Schedule Trigger fires daily at 8AM. The workflow reads your list of target news source URLs from a Google Sheet, then loops through each source one by one.
* **Phase 2 – URL Discovery:** For each news source, the Map Agent crawls the page and extracts individual article URLs. URLs are filtered using include patterns to keep only actual article links, deduplicated, and capped at your configured `maxArticles` limit.
* **Phase 3 – Article Extraction:** Each article URL is processed by the General Agent, which extracts the full content including title, body text, author, and publication date. Articles with fewer than 50 words or that don't mention your defined brand/topic keywords are automatically filtered out and skipped.
* **Phase 4 – AI Sentiment Analysis:** Each relevant article is sent to GPT-4o-mini with a structured prompt. The model returns a complete JSON analysis including sentiment label (positive/neutral/negative), a sentiment score from -1 to 1, a 2–3 sentence summary, key topics, tone classification, the most impactful quote, and whether the article requires an urgent response.
* **Phase 5 – Storage & Reporting:** Every analyzed article is saved as a page in your Notion database with full metadata. If an article is flagged as action-required, an immediate Slack alert is fired. At the end of each run, a Daily Digest is compiled with sentiment breakdowns, average scores, flagged items, and top article summaries — then posted to your Slack channel.

---

## How to Set Up

1. **Create 2 scrapers in your MrScraper account:**
   * Map Agent Scraper (for discovering article URLs from news pages)
   * General Agent Scraper (for extracting full article content: title, body, date, author)
   * Copy the `scraperId` for each.

2. **Enable AI Scraper API access** in your MrScraper account settings.

3. **Prepare your Google Sheet:**
   * Create a sheet with a `Domain` column listing the news source URLs you want to monitor
   * Add as many sources as needed — each will be looped through on every run

4. **Add your credentials in n8n:**
   * MrScraper API token
   * OpenAI API key
   * Slack OAuth
   * Notion OAuth

5. **Configure your Notion database** with these properties:
   * Title, Source URL, Sentiment (select), Sentiment Score (number), Tone (select), Key Topics (text), Summary (text), Action Required (checkbox), Action Reason (text), Published At (text), Scraped At (text)

6. **Update the workflow settings:**
   * Set your `brandName` and `brandKeywords` (comma-separated keywords to filter relevant articles)
   * Set your `mapScraperId` and `generalScraperId`
   * Set your `slackChannel` name (e.g. `#brand-monitoring`)
   * Set your `notionDatabaseId` (from your Notion database URL)
   * Set `maxArticles` to control how many articles are processed per source per run

7. **Adjust the Schedule Trigger** to your preferred run time (default: every 24 hours)

8. **Customize the topic/brand keywords** inside the `Pick Best Content + Filter Irrelevant` code node to match your monitoring targets

---

## Requirements

* **MrScraper** account with API access enabled
* **OpenAI** account (GPT-4o-mini used by default)
* **Slack** workspace with OAuth connected
* **Notion** workspace with a database set up and OAuth connected
* **Google Sheets** (OAuth2 connected) for storing your list of news sources

---

## Good to Know

* GPT-4o-mini is used by default to keep costs low — processing costs approximately $0.0001 per article. Switch to GPT-4o only if you need higher analysis quality.
* The workflow has two distinct Slack outputs: an immediate urgent alert fires the moment a high-priority article is detected, while the Daily Digest summarizes the entire run at the end.
* Articles that are too short (under 50 words) or don't match your defined keywords are silently skipped — the workflow never breaks even if a source returns no usable content.
* Sentiment scores range from -1.0 (strongly negative) to 1.0 (strongly positive), making it easy to track trends over time in Notion or a connected dashboard.

---

## Customising This Workflow

* **Track multiple brands or topics:** Duplicate the keyword filter and AI prompt steps to run parallel analysis pipelines for different subjects in a single workflow.
* **Add email reporting:** Insert a Gmail node after the Slack Daily Digest step to also send the daily summary as a formatted email report.
* **Connect to a dashboard:** Pipe the Notion database into a tool like Google Looker Studio or Retool to visualize sentiment trends over time with charts and filters.
* **Adjust scrape frequency:** Change the Schedule Trigger from daily to hourly for breaking news monitoring, or weekly for slower trend tracking.
* **Expand news sources:** Add Google News search URLs (e.g. `https://news.google.com/search?q=YOUR_BRAND`) to your input sheet for broader coverage beyond specific news sites.

parameters.text:
=Analyze the following news article about the topic / brand "Law".

Article Title: {{ $json.title }}
Published: {{ $json.publishedAt }}
Author: {{ $json.author }}
Source URL: {{ $json.sourceUrl }}

Article Content:
{{ $json.content }}

Return your result in this exact JSON structure:

{
"title",
"published_date",
"author",
"source_url",
"summary",
"sentiment": "positive | neutral | negative",
"sentiment_score": number,
"summary": "string (max 3 sentences)",
"key_topics": ["law"],
"tone": "formal | critical | alarming | investigative | neutral",
"action_required": true or false,
"action_reason": "string",
"quote": "string (most important sentence from article)"
}

Remember:

Output must be valid JSON only.

All fields are required.

Never return null.

Never return markdown.

messageValues.message: You are a news sentiment analysis engine.  You MUST return ONLY valid JSON. Do NOT include markdown. Do NOT include explanations. Do NOT wrap the response in backticks. Do NOT add extra text.  Always return all fields in the schema. If information is missing, use default values.  Sentiment rules:  positive → good news, legal success, improvement  negative → scandal, legal case, corruption, controversy, crime escalation  neutral → factual, informational, balanced  Sentiment score rules:  Range from -1 to 1  Negative = below 0  Neutral = 0 -elopen Positive = above 0

parameters.content:
## Phase 0 — Setup & Configuration

### Before Running This Workflow

**A) MrScraper Setup (required)**
Create TWO scrapers in your MrScraper dashboard:

1. **Map Agent Scraper** — discovers article URLs from news/search pages
   - Copy its `scraperId` → paste into `mapScraperId` in Workflow Config

2. **General Agent Scraper** — extracts full article content (title, body, date, author)
   - Target fields: `title`, `content` or `text` or `markdown`, `published_at`, `author`
   - Copy its `scraperId` → paste into `generalScraperId` in Workflow Config
3. **Enable AI Scraper API access** in your MrScraper account (so n8n can run scrapers via API).


**B) Credentials Needed in n8n**
- `MrScraper` API credential → connect to both MrScraper nodes.
- `OpenAI` API credential → connect to OpenAI Chat Model.
- `Slack` OAuth credential → connect to both Slack nodes.
- `Notion` OAuth credential → connect to Notion node.

**C) Workflow Config Node — Fill in these values:**
- `brandName`: your brand (e.g. `Anthropic`)
- `brandKeywords`: comma-separated keywords (e.g. `Anthropic, Claude, claude.ai`)
- `newsUrls`: comma-separated news search URLs to scrape
- `mapScraperId` + `generalScraperId`: from MrScraper
- `slackChannel`: Slack channel name (e.g. `#brand-monitoring`)
- `notionDatabaseId`: your Notion database ID (from the URL)
- `maxArticles`: max articles to analyze per run (default: 10)

**D) Notion Database Setup**
Create a Notion database with these properties (optional):
- `Title` (title)
- `Source URL` (URL)
- `Sentiment` (select: positive / neutral / negative)
- `Sentiment Score` (number)
- `Tone` (select)
- `Key Topics` (rich text)
- `Summary` (rich text)
- `Brand Mentions` (number)
- `Action Required` (checkbox)
- `Action Reason` (rich text)
- `Published At` (rich text)
- `Scraped At` (rich text)

### What To Do
* Set the workflow to run **daily at 8:00 AM** (or adjust to your preferred time).
* Configure all global variables:
  brand name, keywords, scraper IDs,
  Slack channel, and Notion database ID.
* Provide the list of news source URLs.
* Ensure the URLs are split into individual items
  so they can be processed in parallel.

### Key Settings to Change
* **Trigger time** → edit the *Schedule* node.
* **Brand & integration config** → edit the **Workflow Config** node.

### What To Do
1. Run **Map Agent (Rerun)** using your domain as input (Please enter the maximum depth, maximum pages, and include pattern (includePattern) as needed.)
2. Extract and clean the URLs:
   * Remove tag, category, and author pages
   * Remove non-http(s) links
   * Deduplicate results
   * Limit the output to `maxArticles` per run
3. Add a fallback condition:
   * If no valid URLs are found, return a *skip item* instead of stopping the workflow.

### Tips
* Use Google News search URLs for broader coverage, for example:
  [https://news.google.com/search?q=YOUR_BRAND](https://news.google.com/search?q=YOUR_BRAND) or YOUR_TOPIC
* You can also define specific URL patterns from the target news site to avoid collecting unwanted links.
* Adjust `maxArticles` to balance speed and processing cost.

### What To Do
1. For each URL, call the **General Agent (Rerun)** using the scraperId from the mrscraper platform that was previously created to scrape each article URL
   and extract the full content (title, body, date, author).
2. You can adjust TopicBrandKeywords to ensure the article is relevant to your topic / brand
3. Skip any article with fewer than 50 word.
4. Skip any article that does not mention your defined brand keywords.
5. Ensure all `skip=true` items continue through the workflow safely (do not break execution).

### Expected Output Per Article (adjust according to the scraper output you have already built)
* `title`
* `article` (trimmed to a maximum of 4000 characters)
* `publishedAt`
* `author`
* `sourceUrl`
* Include the configuration data so it can be used in the LLM step.

### What To Do
1. Send each processed article to **GPT-4o-mini**
   using a structured analysis prompt.
2. Define the **system prompt directly inside the OpenAI Chat Model node**
   to ensure `chainLlm` correctly passes the instructions.
3. Configure the model to return structured JSON containing:
   * `sentiment` (positive / neutral / negative)
   * `sentiment_score` (-1.0 to 1.0)
   * `summary` (2–3 sentences)
   * `key_topics` (array of detected topics)
   * `tone` (praise / criticism / neutral / etc.)
   * `action_required` (true if the brand should respond)
   * `action_reason` (explanation if action is needed)
   * `quote` (most impactful quote from the article)
4. Update the Slack formatting logic:
   * Remove the `| upper` Jinja filter
   * Use JavaScript `.toUpperCase()` instead
5. Add a condition:
   * If `action_required = true`, send an **immediate Slack alert**.

### Cost Tip
* Use `gpt-4o-mini` for low-cost processing (around ~$0.0001 per article).
* Switch to `gpt-4o` only if higher analysis quality is required.

### What To Do
1. Configure **Notion** to save every analyzed article
   as a new database page with complete metadata.
   * Ensure all Notion property `textValue` fields are properly mapped and populated.
2. Build a **Daily Digest** aggregation step that:
   * Counts total analyzed articles
   * Calculates sentiment breakdown (positive / neutral / negative)
   * Computes average sentiment score
   * Lists all flagged action items
   * Selects the top 5 article summaries
   * Connects to the **Loop Each Article URL → done output (output 0)** to ensure proper execution order.
3. Configure **Slack Daily Digest** to send the final summary report
   to your Slack channel at the end of the workflow.
   * Add an empty-run fallback so the digest still sends
     even if 0 articles were processed.

### Slack Alert Types
*  **Urgent Alert** — Trigger immediately when
  `action_required = true`.
*  **Daily Digest** — Send once at the end of the run
  with a complete summary of all processed articles.

### Customize
* Update Notion database properties directly in the Notion node.
* Modify Slack message formatting inside the Code node.
* Optionally add a Gmail node after the Slack Digest step
  to send the report via email.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
